lifecycle.name: IMPLEMENTATION
lifecycle.summary: Executes the approved plan. The orchestrator dispatches implementation sub-tasks using the dispatch mode determined by the runtime (see CLAUDE.md dispatch-mode). In agent-teams mode, teammates coordinate via async messages. In sub-agent mode, each Task call blocks until the sub-agent completes and returns its result.

actions.action: Read the approved plan, ticket ID, ticket issue_type, and base branch
Initialize the dispatch mode's infrastructure (create Agent Team if agent-teams mode, or proceed directly if sub-agent mode).
Read the plan's sub-task list and dependency ordering
For each sub-task, determine the model tier using the planner's complexity rating (see MODELS_AND_ROLES.xml dispatch-override): - light → haiku - standard → sonnet - complex → opus
Dispatch independent sub-tasks in parallel using the active dispatch mode
Dispatch dependent sub-tasks only after their dependencies complete
Each sub-task receives: - The sub-task description, files to modify, acceptance criteria - The branch to commit to - The ticket ID for commit messages - The relevant behavior files for their role
Collect results from all dispatched sub-tasks as they complete
Track: success/failure, files modified, commits made
For failed sub-tasks, collect the structured failure report (escalation behavior)

phase.name: EVALUATE

actions.action: Assess the outcome of every sub-task: determine whether each completed successfully or produced a failure report
Classify any failures as TECHNICAL (logic bug, command error, test failure, syntax issue) or REQUIREMENTS (impossible plan, unclear spec, wrong direction)

phase.on-success: All sub-tasks succeeded → proceed to HUMAN_GATE
phase.on-failure: Classify the failure as TECHNICAL or REQUIREMENTS. TECHNICAL: spawn an Opus sub-agent with the failure context. If resolved, loop to DISPATCH for remaining work. If Opus also fails, present to human. REQUIREMENTS: present to the human for clarification.

phase.name: HUMAN_GATE

actions.action: Present implementation results for approval (see human-gate behavior, context: IMPLEMENTATION_REVIEW)

phase.on-rejection: Pass feedback and dispatch corrective sub-tasks → loop to DISPATCH

phase.name: PUSH
phase.actor: Orchestrator launches a push agent (light tier)

actions.action: Push the branch to remote: git push -u origin {branch-name}
Confirm the push succeeded before transitioning

phase.note: This is the only push during implementation. Sub-tasks never push directly. The push happens after human approval so unapproved work never reaches the remote.

actions.action: If agent-teams dispatch mode was used: send shutdown_request to all teammates, wait for acknowledgment, then call TeamDelete to clean up the team.
